items.title: Critical Remote Code Execution in Amasty Extensions
items.summary: Amasty released a massive security update for 25 Magento extensions, with one critical vulnerability that could lead to remote code execution. Sansec Shield already blocks the critical issues.

items.title: Unauthenticated File Upload in Amasty Order Attributes for Magento
items.summary: An unauthenticated arbitrary file upload flaw in Amasty Order Attributes allows attackers to write executable files to a Magento store without logging in, potentially leading to remote code execution.

items.title: Unauthenticated PHP Object Injection in Mirasvit Cache Warmer
items.summary: Sansec discovered a critical unauthenticated PHP object injection vulnerability in Mirasvit Cache Warmer, a popular Magento extension, which can lead to remote code execution via a crafted cookie. CISA has added this flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog due to active exploitation.

items.title: Unrestricted File Upload in Magento and Adobe Commerce via REST API
items.summary: Sansec identified PolyShell, a critical vulnerability in Magento's REST API that enables unauthenticated attackers to upload executable files disguised as images, potentially leading to remote code execution or account takeover. Attacks have been observed against a significant percentage of stores.

items.title: Security Update for Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source
items.summary: Adobe released a critical security update addressing multiple vulnerabilities in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, which could lead to security feature bypass, privilege escalation, arbitrary code execution, and arbitrary file system read. Adobe recommends immediate application of the patches.
